documents.text: Machine learning algorithms learn patterns from data using statistical methods
Deep learning neural networks require large training datasets for supervised learning
Unsupervised learning discovers hidden structure in unlabeled data
Reinforcement learning agents maximize cumulative reward through exploration
Transfer learning adapts pre-trained models to new domains with limited data
Information retrieval systems search and rank documents by relevance to queries
BM25 is a bag of words retrieval function that ranks documents based on term frequency
TF-IDF weighting reflects how important a word is to a document in a collection
Query expansion improves search recall by adding related terms to the original query
Relevance feedback uses explicit user judgments to improve retrieval performance
Relational databases store data in tables with SQL as the query language
NoSQL databases provide flexible schema design for unstructured data storage
Database indexing structures like B-trees accelerate data retrieval operations
Transaction processing ensures ACID properties for reliable data operations
Distributed databases partition data across multiple nodes for scalability
Vector search uses embedding similarity to find semantically related documents
Hybrid search combines lexical matching with vector similarity for better retrieval
Bayesian probability provides a framework for updating beliefs with new evidence
Probabilistic models estimate relevance scores using statistical inference methods
Cosine similarity measures the angle between two vectors in high-dimensional space
